name: Bridgeport Post Office
description_short: Federal credit union serving postal employees and families with auto loans, personal loans, credit cards, and prepaid debit cards.
description_long:
Bridgeport Post Office Federal Credit Union is a member-owned, not-for-profit financial institution based in Bridgeport, Connecticut, operating since at least the mid-20th century. The credit union is NCUA-insured and primarily serves postal employees and their immediate and extended families, including spouses, children, grandchildren, parents, grandparents, and siblings. The organization maintains a physical office with limited hours reflecting a small-scale operation focused on personalized service to a specific membership community.

The credit union offers traditional financial products including auto loans (as low as 2.90% APR), personal loans (as low as 8.00% APR), share certificates, and VISA-branded credit and debit products. They provide VISA Platinum credit cards starting at 9.90% APR, CU Money prepaid debit cards with $6.95 setup fees, and VISA gift cards with $5.00 activation fees. Members can access online banking 24/7 to view account histories, and funds loaded to prepaid cards are available within two hours. The credit union also processes ACH transactions and offers first-time borrower loan programs to help establish credit.

Bridgeport Post Office FCU distinguishes itself through exclusive membership based on postal employment and family relationships, making it highly specialized rather than community-based. The extremely limited business hours (closed Wed-Sun, with only morning or afternoon slots available Mon-Tue-Thu-Fri) reflect a volunteer or minimal-staff operation. Their emphasis on prepaid debit cards for out-of-state and student members, combined with semi-annual mailing of statements, suggests adaptation to members who cannot visit the physical office. The partnership with VISA enables product offerings uncommon in very small credit unions.

This credit union is genuinely limited in scope and accessibility compared to larger financial institutions. The restrictive field of membership makes it unavailable to the general public. While loan rates are competitive, the extremely limited office hours and geographic location in Bridgeport create practical barriers even for eligible members. The semi-annual statement mailing and reliance on online banking suggest the institution is transitioning away from paper-based services, which may disadvantage less tech-savvy members.
services: VISA Platinum credit cards (9.90% APR starting rate)
First-time borrower loan programs for credit establishment
CU Money prepaid debit cards ($6.95 setup, 2-hour load time)
Online banking and account history access
Check services (eliminated in-person check requests; online alternative)
Membership for postal employees and extended families
pros: Auto loan rates as low as 2.90% APR—highly competitive for credit union members
Personal loan rates starting at 8.00% APR for installment borrowing and consolidation
VISA Platinum credit cards with APR starting at 9.90%, paired with first-time borrower programs
CU Money prepaid debit cards load funds within 2 hours, useful for out-of-state and remote members
24/7 online banking access to view full account histories and manage accounts independently
Exclusive membership restricted to postal employees and families creates a tight-knit community
NCUA insurance protection on deposits up to $250,000 per account category
cons: Extremely limited office hours (closed Wed-Sun; only 7:30am-12pm Mon/Thu and 10am-3pm Tue/Fri) make in-person service nearly inaccessible
Restrictive field of membership—only postal employees and immediate/extended family eligible; closed to general public
Small institution with minimal staffing suggests limited product depth, investment options, and customer service capacity
Semi-annual statement mailing and heavy reliance on online banking may alienate less tech-comfortable members
$6.95 setup fee for prepaid debit cards and $5.00 fee for gift card activation add friction to basic services
best_for: Postal employees and their families seeking competitive auto and personal loan rates
Out-of-state or remote members who need prepaid debit card access without visiting a physical branch
Individuals building or establishing credit through first-time borrower loan programs within the postal community
Members who prefer community-focused, member-owned financial institutions over large commercial banks
diagnosis: Bridgeport Post Office FCU is best for postal employees and their families seeking competitive loan rates and basic credit union services in a member-focused environment. The main caveat is that membership is restricted exclusively to postal workers and their relatives, and the severely limited office hours (closed Wed-Sun, with only half-day slots other days) make in-person access impractical for most members, effectively requiring reliance on online banking and phone support.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
meta_description: Bridgeport Post Office — NCUA-insured credit union in Bridgeport, CT. Member-owned, not-for-profit. Loans, savings, and member services.
